scenario: Nimbus Healthcare runs a Spring Boot client on Camunda 8.8 against a self-managed cluster authenticated via OIDC. The platform team must set the resource value that the access token returned by the authorization server is required to be valid for, so the broker accepts 4500 jobs per hour from the worker fleet without rejecting the token.
question: Which Spring Boot property names the resource that the OIDC access token must be valid for in a Camunda 8.8 client configuration?

options.text: Set camunda.client.auth.audience to the resource identifier the access token must be valid for when authenticating the Camunda client against the OIDC provider
Set camunda.client.auth.scope to the resource identifier the access token must be valid for when authenticating the Camunda client against the OIDC provider
Set camunda.client.auth.resource to the resource identifier the access token must be valid for when authenticating the Camunda client against the OIDC provider
Set camunda.client.auth.client-id to the resource identifier the access token must be valid for when authenticating the Camunda client against the OIDC provider

a.text: Correct. camunda.client.auth.audience names the resource the access token must be valid for; a default is set by camunda.client.mode saas combined with camunda.client.auth.method oidc.

b.text: Incorrect. Scope describes which permissions an access token carries; the resource the token must be valid for is configured via camunda.client.auth.audience in the Spring Boot starter.

c.text: Incorrect. The documented key is named audience, not resource; the Camunda 8.8 Spring Boot starter does not expose a camunda.client.auth.resource key under client auth.

d.text: Incorrect. camunda.client.auth.client-id identifies the client when requesting a token from the authorization server; the target resource is named via camunda.client.auth.audience.

explanation: camunda.client.auth.audience holds the resource value that the OIDC access token must be valid for. A default is applied when camunda.client.mode is saas and camunda.client.auth.method is oidc. The client-id property identifies the requesting client, not the protected resource.
